behavior_summary: U8P_WATER4::gotHit is a small process run when the actor/object is struck; it immediately delegates all hit-response behavior to a WATER-specific handler (WATER::func0080) and then suspends.
The script does not modify global flags or maintain local state in this excerpt; it serves as a thin delegator to water-specific logic implemented elsewhere.
Parameters to U8P_WATER4::gotHit are present but not used in a visible way here; their meaning is inferred from typical U8P_WATER4::gotHit signatures (source id, damage/flags).
notable_control_flow: U8P_WATER4::gotHit executes on a hit event, calls WATER::func0080, then issues a suspend/terminates its process.
There are no conditional branches, menu interactions, or flag reads/writes in this snippet — linear delegate-then-suspend flow.

Are the U8P_WATER4::gotHit parameters (param1, param2) forwarded into WATER::func0080 in the full implementation, or ignored by the handler?
Is there any higher-level state or global flags elsewhere that gate or respond to WATER::func0080's effects?
